cover.title: the most expensive thing your agents do is re-read your codebase
cover.sub: no durable memory means they reconstruct context on every single run. here's the bill.

slides.title: the tax
slides.body: with no durable memory, every agent run rebuilds context from zero. multiply by 30 developers' worth of agent runs and that reconstruction is most of your bill, and most of your latency.

slides.title: the fix
slides.body: a canonical context layer: one current answer instead of a re-read. first-party, our own run.

slides.title: it's not really a cost problem
slides.body: context is a reliability problem wearing a cost problem's clothes. the agent acting on stale context ships the wrong call. the bill is just the part you noticed first.

cta.title: how agents should remember your codebase
cta.sub: once, correctly, and shared. not from scratch, every run.
